element_introduction_order.mastery_context: balance and timing in contained vertical space
speed control and collision avoidance on linear track
air control and landing precision with high consequences
gate navigation and speed management on descent
aiming, resource management, and opponent prediction

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: discrete event-based pacing with multiplayer turn-taking; tension builds during individual performance window then releases at event completion

difficulty_curve.early_game: snowboard halfpipe and sled racing introduce basic directional control and timing without extreme penalty
difficulty_curve.mid_game: aerial skiing introduces precision landing requirement with high failure consequence
difficulty_curve.late_game: downhill skiing combines gate navigation with speed management; snowball fights add strategic opponent interaction
notable_spikes: aerial skiing jump sequences requiring precise air control
downhill skiing gate gates at high speed
multiplayer competitive pressure in hotseat modes

notable_level_patterns.description: Five discrete competitive events (halfpipe, sled racing, aerial, downhill, snowball fights) playable individually or in sequential tournament mode with up to six players in hotseat competition
notable_level_patterns.example_level: all minigames follow event-based structure rather than progressive level design

notable_level_patterns.description: Three events (aerial, downhill, halfpipe) center on downward movement through vertically-oriented spaces with increasing speed and danger
notable_level_patterns.example_level: aerial skiing and downhill skiing events

notable_level_patterns.description: Four of five minigames support sequential multiplayer via hotseat; one supports semi-hotseat (two players simultaneous); tension/relief cycles through player turns
notable_level_patterns.example_level: snowball fights support full hotseat rotation
